author: SuperSoft
content:
We've been working with a local fab shop on a set of adjustable control arms for one of our Tiburons and I figured I'd share what we we find along the way.

The car is on Ksport Kontrol Pro coilovers with adjustable sway links. They solved a lot of problems but created some new ones too. With the ride height dropped, the stock arms ended up pointing uphill, which drags the roll center way down and makes the front end a little unpredictable. Part of the plan with these arms is to get that geometry back where it should be so the car behaves a bit more like it's supposed to.

[IMAGE 1: Engine Nut Automotive Fuel System Fastener Shock absorber]
[IMAGE 2: Synthetic rubber]
[IMAGE 3: control arm photo]

The main thing we wanted, though, was caster adjustment. The SuperPro offset bushings only get you so far, and cutting into the strut towers was the other option. Adjustable arms should give us the freedom to move things around and let us ditch camber bolts now that we're already pushing past what the KSport plates allow.

[IMAGE 4: control arm detail]

Everything's being switched over to heims instead of rubber bushings and stock ball joints too, which should keep the alignment from wandering and hold up better over long stints. I'm also incapable of using a grease gun without greasing myself so that's a plus too.

[IMAGE 5: heim joint detail]
[IMAGE 6: heim joint detail]

We dropped off a set of moog arms for the jig and honestly, they're not great out of the box.

[IMAGE 7: moog arm photo]
[IMAGE 8: moog arm photo]

author: chase206
content: Any chance they want to fab up more for sale? These could be a hot item for the low boys!

author: ardamir
content:
Running H&R lowering springs and being really disappointed on how the car behaves (especially the front end) during track use, this post explains a whole lot on why this is happening.

I'm waiting for my coilovers to arrive in a month, I would seriously consider these control arms too.
Really interested to see how this goes!

content: You believe the factory softer bushings cause a issue when lowered?

author: SuperSoft
content: He doesn't love doing repetitive stuff but he said he'd be up for doing a few. We'll have the jig after if anyone does want to make a bunch of them or we can just share a parts list & measurements.
